Angular 22 will not break your app.
It will break something harder to fix.
Your muscle memory.
The upgrade itself is almost boring. Run the migration, watch the CLI politely label your old components, ship. Nothing burns. And that is exactly why this release is dangerous in a quiet way: everything still works, so you can keep writing Angular the way you wrote it in 2024, on top of a framework that has moved on without you.
I upgraded a mid-size production app in the first week after the June release. Total migration effort: one afternoon. Total habits I caught myself repeating that the framework had just made obsolete: seven. Every single one still compiled. Every single one was now the old way.
This article is that list. Not a changelog summary, there are enough of those. This is a habit audit: seven reflexes you have built over years of Angular, what version 22 replaced them with, and the muscle memory you need to overwrite. Your app will survive the upgrade either way. The question is whether your habits do.
Habit 1: Adding OnPush Yourself (It Is Now the Default)
For years, the senior move was typing changeDetection…
